description: This figure presents a Time-Temperature-Transformation (TTT) diagram illustrating the decomposition kinetics of γ-solid solution in an Fe-28Mn-8.5Al-1C-1.25Si alloy during isothermal aging. The diagram maps temperature (300–950°C) against logarithmic time (0.01–1000 h), delineating five distinct transformation zones: Area 1 represents the single-phase austenite (γ) region where spinodal decomposition initiates; Area 2 corresponds to homogeneous precipitation of κ′-carbide within the γ matrix (γ+κ′); Area 3 indicates grain boundary precipitation of κ*-carbide (γ+κ′+κ*); Area 4 depicts discontinuous reaction products comprising grain boundary α-ferrite with B2/DO₃ ordering (γ+κ′+κ*+α(B2/DO₃)); and Area 5 shows the equilibrium phase assemblage of γ+κ+B2/DO₃. The C-shaped curves reveal that aging below 500°C significantly extends the time required for grain boundary κ* precipitation, while intermediate temperatures (600–800°C) accelerate carbide formation. The diagram demonstrates that Si addition promotes B2/DO₃ precipitate formation while suppressing β-Mn phase, providing critical kinetic boundaries for controlling microstructural evolution during heat treatment of austenitic low-density steels.
